meta.name: RCADS-25 Revised Child Anxiety and Depression Scale
meta.fullName: Revised Child Anxiety and Depression Scale - 25 Item
meta.description: The RCADS-25 is a 25-item short form assessing anxiety and depression symptoms in children and adolescents.
meta.author: Ebesutani C, et al. (2012)
meta.reference: Ebesutani C, et al. The Revised Child Anxiety and Depression Scale-Short Version: Scale reduction via exploratory bifactor modeling of the broad anxiety factor. Psychol Assess. 2012;24(4):833-845.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how often each item is true for you based on how you have been feeling recently.

questions.text: I worry about things.
When I have a problem, my heart beats really fast.
When I have a problem, I feel scared.
I worry that bad things will happen to me.
When I have a problem, I feel shaky.
I suddenly feel really scared for no reason at all.
I worry that I will suddenly get a scared feeling when there is nothing to be afraid of.
I am afraid of being in crowded places.
I worry about looking foolish in front of people.
I worry that I will do something to make a fool of myself.
I have to keep checking that I have done things right.
I am scared to go to school.
I worry what other people think of me.
I am afraid of talking in front of the class.
I am afraid of being alone.
Nothing is much fun anymore.
I feel sad or empty.
I am not interested in anything.
I have problems with my schoolwork.
I cannot do anything well.
I feel worthless.
I feel tired a lot.
I feel restless.
I have problems with my sleep.
I have no energy for things.

ranges.label: Normal Range
ranges.description: Your total score on the RCADS-25 falls within the normal range, indicating that your anxiety and depression symptoms are not clinically concerning at this time. You may experience occasional worries or low mood, but these are within the typical range of emotional experiences for your age group.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current healthy habits including regular physical activity, adequate sleep, and social connections
Be aware that occasional stress and worry are normal parts of life and do not necessarily indicate a problem
If you notice a significant increase in anxiety or sadness in the future, consider re-taking this assessment to monitor any changes

ranges.label: Elevated Symptoms
ranges.description: Your RCADS-25 score indicates elevated anxiety and/or depression symptoms that exceed the typical range. These symptoms may be causing noticeable distress or beginning to interfere with your daily activities, school performance, or social relationships. Early attention to these symptoms is advisable.
suggestions: Talk to a trusted adult such as a parent, teacher, or school counselor about how you have been feeling recently
Maintain regular exercise and consistent sleep habits, as these have strong evidence for reducing both anxiety and depressive symptoms
Practice relaxation techniques such as deep breathing, progressive muscle relaxation, or mindfulness exercises when feeling anxious
Consider scheduling an appointment with a school counselor or mental health professional for a more thorough evaluation

ranges.label: Clinically Significant
ranges.description: Your score on the RCADS-25 reaches clinically significant levels, suggesting that anxiety and/or depression symptoms are substantially impacting your daily functioning, well-being, and quality of life. Professional evaluation and intervention are strongly recommended to help you manage these symptoms effectively.
suggestions: Please consult a qualified mental health professional such as a psychologist or psychiatrist as soon as possible for a comprehensive clinical evaluation
Do not carry this burden alone; share your feelings with trusted family members, friends, or school staff who can provide support
A professional assessment can help determine the most appropriate evidence-based intervention plan, which may include cognitive behavioral therapy
If you experience thoughts of self-harm or hopelessness, please reach out to a crisis helpline or emergency services immediately
Remember that anxiety and depression are highly treatable conditions, and seeking help is a sign of strength, not weakness

anxiety.label: Normal
anxiety.description: Your anxiety subscale score is within the normal range. You may experience occasional worries or nervousness, but these feelings are typical and do not significantly disrupt your daily functioning or well-being.

anxiety.label: Elevated
anxiety.description: Your anxiety subscale score is elevated, indicating that anxiety symptoms such as excessive worry, physical tension, panic feelings, or social fears are noticeably present and may be affecting your concentration, sleep, or social interactions.

anxiety.label: Clinical
anxiety.description: Your anxiety subscale score is in the clinically significant range, suggesting persistent and intense anxiety symptoms that are substantially interfering with your daily life. Professional evaluation focusing on anxiety management strategies is strongly recommended.

depression.label: Normal
depression.description: Your depression subscale score is within the normal range. You generally maintain interest in activities, adequate energy levels, and a positive sense of self-worth in your daily life.

depression.label: Elevated
depression.description: Your depression subscale score is elevated, suggesting symptoms such as persistent sadness, loss of interest in previously enjoyed activities, fatigue, or feelings of worthlessness that may be impacting your mood and daily engagement.

depression.label: Clinical
depression.description: Your depression subscale score is in the clinically significant range, indicating pervasive depressive symptoms that are likely causing substantial distress and functional impairment. A professional evaluation for depression is strongly recommended.
